title: cassine confertiflora
intro: Cassine confertiflora, also known as the bitter cassia, is a shrub-like plant native to tropical Africa, belonging to the Fabaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Cassine confertiflora include relieving digestive issues, reducing fever, and supporting the immune system. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be consumed in capsule or powder form after drying and processing.
